name: Dick Vance
abstract: Dick Vance (November 28, 1915 – July 31, 1985) was an American jazz trumpeter and arranger.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to Ella Fitzgerald
Newport Jazz Festival: The Ellington Saga, Part II
Kool Jazz Festival: A Salute to Count Basie

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: I had Dick Vance, I had Trummy, I had Al Killian... in the band with me there.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: That was my rhythm section. I got an excellent little trumpet player named Dick Vance on trumpet.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And then I don't Rex Stewart was there then. They got a little guy there -- Dick Vance, was he there? [INTERVIEWEE] No. This is a nice-looking little stark fellow and he played pretty good.

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Johnson Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: I liked Don Redman’s band. I thought that Don Redman’s band was like perfect almost, like you know, because you know with Taft Jordan, Dick Vance and all those great trumpet players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Johnson Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Eldridge
about_quotes.evidence: We was just going... I got married that same day, too. ... We told the conductor... because we was in the coach. ... Dick Vance was there.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: I got Dick Vance, and we had a little drummer named Kenny... I did some records with Dick Vance and Benny Morton on my own
